title: [lichen_l2.c:284] replay_table not cleared before lichen_link_init at line 1309
description:
Completeness: Initialization ordering category.

At line 1309, lichen_replay_table_init(&replay_table) is called AFTER lichen_link_init(&link_ctx, eui64) at line 1308.

The link_ctx initialization does not depend on replay_table, so the order is functionally correct. However:

1. In the re-init path (lines 1295-1306), cleanup happens before re-init
2. But replay_table_init at 1309 happens outside the mutex-protected cleanup block

If there were any async access to replay_table between link_ctx init (1308) and replay_table init (1309), it could see partially-initialized state. This is unlikely given the initialization ordering, but the asymmetry with the cleanup path (which holds mutexes) is notable.

This is informational - the code is correct, but the initialization sequence could be made more obviously safe by initializing replay_table while still holding mutexes if any are held.
close_reason: Added comment clarifying init ordering is safe: no async access before link_ctx_initialized is set
